components.title: Map Content
components.description: Choose point, line, area, or container components and preview them directly on the map.

map.googleKeyMissing: VITE_MAP_KEY is missing, so Google Maps cannot load
map.loadError: Google Maps failed to load. Check the API key or network

useCase.status: Package has reached the final destination

manual.title: Component Manual
manual.intro: Organize map component guidance by map type, component category, and concrete component for long-term expansion.

mapTypeDescriptions.entity: Real streets and business POI contexts
mapTypeDescriptions.visualization: Muted base map focused on data expression

manual.usageIntro: Tags summarize the scenario type; detailed guidance lives in the bullets.

placeholders.componentDescription: Add the real component name, preview, style, FakeMod, and implementation notes after design import.
placeholders.pendingBullet: Fill this after Figma MCP or component design data is connected.

pointMarker.name: Normal
pointMarker.description: Point component for location, muted, completed, emphasized, and selected states on entity maps.

style.variantSet: 4 base states distributed on the map: default / muted / completed / emphasized; hover&selected is the interaction state
style.sizes: 24px for base states; 40px for emphasized and hover/selected states
style.color: Blue for default, emphasized, and selected; gray for muted and completed, then blue emphasized styling on selection
style.shadow: White stroke plus subtle shadow for legibility on dense base maps

usage.primary: Represent user location, destination points, POI, or key logistics trace nodes.
usage.extended: Use emphasized or selected states for high-priority nodes when the map needs a clear current focus.
usage.constraints: Avoid using emphasized states for many points in the same viewport so the base map, routes, and key POI remain readable.

informatedLocation.name: Informated location
informatedLocation.description: Information-rich location point with a warehouse icon for business entities on the map.

style.variantSet: 4 base states distributed on the map: default / Disable / completed / emphasized; hover&selected is the interaction state
style.sizes: Rendered at original SVG bounds: 59x62 for base states; 95x99 for emphasized and hover/selected states
style.color: Blue for default, emphasized, and selected; Disable uses #C9D2FC; completed uses neutral gray
style.icon: White stroked warehouse line icon, centered
style.shadow: Use the SVG's built-in white stroke and shadow to match the Figma export

usage.primary: Use for warehouses, stores, stations, or locations with explicit business meaning.
usage.extended: Use emphasized or selected states for selected warehouses, active delivery nodes, or high-priority operation points.
usage.constraints: Information-rich points carry more visual weight than normal points; control density when many are visible in the same viewport.

cumulativeLocation.name: Cumulative location
cumulativeLocation.description: Cluster location component for lower zoom levels. It progressively splits as users zoom in until the leaf layer becomes Normal points.

style.variantSet: Cluster / Default; hover&selected switches to Cluster / Hover; high zoom splits into Normal points
style.sizes: 40px default state; 64px hover/selected state; the number changes with cluster count, and leaf nodes use Normal sizing
style.color: #2450FF blue center + white stroke + white number
style.shadow: White outer ring and subtle shadow for map legibility

usage.primary: Group nearby Normal or Informated location points when the map is zoomed out.
usage.extended: Progressively split by the current zoom level, for example 42 becomes 10 + 32, then 3 + 7 + 12 + 20.
usage.constraints: Cluster radius should adapt to zoom; once the leaf layer is reached, render Normal points instead of numbered bubbles.

customLocation.name: Custom
customLocation.description: Custom location component for expressing custom point types with letters, numbers, or business icons.

style.variantSet: 5 content variants: letter / number / warehouse / shop / user
style.sizes: 40px component bounds; icon SVGs render with an overflowing 59x62 shadow frame
style.color: #2A3EF4 blue center with white shape, icon, or text
style.icon: Supports letter, number, and business icon slots
style.shadow: White pin base plus subtle shadow for legibility on dense maps

fakeMod.property: Property 1 = Location / With info / {letter | number | warehouse | shop | user}

usage.primary: Use for point components that need business-specific icons, shapes, or content.
usage.extended: Can become a future template entry for stores, campaign locations, exception points, or custom operational POI.
usage.constraints: Control the number of custom content types in one map view so letters, numbers, and icons do not become hard to scan.

style.sizes: Preview group is about 282x121; route body is 8px and increases to 12px on hover/selected
style.shadow: Routes use a white outer stroke and subtle shadow; overlapping segments are expressed by layered primary and secondary routes

usage.primary: Use for delivery, navigation, inspection, or trace replay paths where A/B origins merge into one destination.
usage.constraints: Avoid stacking many highlighted routes in one viewport; reserve hover/selected for the focused route.

normalRouteNoArrow.name: Normal route / No arrow
normalRouteNoArrow.description: Regular route without directional arrows, used when path presence matters more than direction.

style.variantSet: Line / no arrow: default / muted / completed / hover&selected

usage.extended: Use for historical traces, planned paths, boundary connections, or route displays where direction is not important.

normalRouteHasArrow.name: Normal route / Has arrow
normalRouteHasArrow.description: Regular route with directional arrows, used to express route direction, flow, or next movement.

style.variantSet: Line / has arrow: default / muted / completed / hover&selected
style.arrow: Repeated arrow mask inside the route body; arrow color follows the route state

usage.extended: Use for delivery direction, the next navigation segment, vehicle/package flow, or paths where start/end direction matters.

trackingBubble.casePrimary: Show the current package node, delivery point, or exception point in a logistics trace.
trackingBubble.caseSecondary: Highlight a destination or next action in mobility, food delivery, and local courier flows.
trackingBubble.caseConstraint: Prefer one full-information bubble per viewport to avoid covering routes and key POI.

marker.casePrimary: Mark a warehouse, store, driver, or user location as a single map entity.
marker.caseSecondary: Pair with labels or clustering to represent multiple nearby POI.

routeLine.casePrimary: Represent delivery, navigation, inspection, or tracking path direction.
routeLine.caseSecondary: Use dashed styling for predicted paths, historical traces, or unconfirmed routes.
